test.name: Exhaustiveness evaluation request should not trigger question-selection
test.description: User wants to evaluate whether enough searching has been done to declare a question answered. That is research-exhaustiveness — question-selection adds new questions, research-exhaustiveness evaluates whether existing ones are done.

input.user_message: Have we searched enough to declare the parentage question done? Can we say we're finished?

negative.explanation: The user is asking whether research on an existing question is reasonably exhaustive — the SKILL.md explicitly prohibits question-selection from evaluating exhaustiveness. That judgment belongs to research-exhaustiveness, which applies the GPS stop criteria.

judge_context: Should route to research-exhaustiveness rather than add a new question or opine on whether the current research is sufficient
